what: 1. Bounded proportions and the simplex distribution
title: Fast simplex regression with variable dispersion
text: Responses continuous proportions rates restricted open interval (0,1)(0, 1) — fraction resource used, recovery rate, concentration relative maximum — violate assumptions ordinary linear regression: support bounded variance inherently heteroscedastic, shrinking towards boundaries 00 11. Two likelihood based models dominate setting: beta regression simplex regression. package implements latter. simplex distribution (Barndorff-Nielsen Jørgensen, 1991) member class dispersion models. random variable Y∈(0,1)Y \(0, 1) simplex distribution mean μ∈(0,1)\mu \(0, 1) dispersion ϕ>0\phi > 0, written Y∼Simplex(μ,ϕ)Y \sim \mathrm{Simplex}(\mu, \phi), density f(y;μ,ϕ)=[2πϕ{y(1−y)}3]−1/2exp⁡{−12ϕd(y;μ)},d(y;μ)=(y−μ)2y(1−y)μ2(1−μ)2, f(y; \mu, \phi) = \bigl[2\pi\,\phi\,\{y(1-y)\}^3\bigr]^{-1/2}   \exp\!\left\{ -\frac{1}{2\phi}\, d(y; \mu) \right\}, \qquad d(y; \mu) = \frac{(y-\mu)^2}{y(1-y)\,\mu^2(1-\mu)^2},  d(y;μ)d(y; \mu) unit deviance. log scale, log⁡f(y;μ,ϕ)=−12(log⁡2π+log⁡ϕ)−32(log⁡y+log⁡(1−y))−12ϕd(y;μ). \log f(y; \mu, \phi) = -\tfrac12\bigl(\log 2\pi + \log\phi\bigr)   - \tfrac32\bigl(\log y + \log(1-y)\bigr) - \tfrac{1}{2\phi}\, d(y; \mu).  mean YY exactly μ\mu, first order dispersion variance Var(Y)≈ϕV(μ)\mathrm{Var}(Y) \approx \phi\, V(\mu) simplex variance function V(μ)=μ3(1−μ)3V(\mu) = \mu^3(1-\mu)^3 — variance largest near μ=1/2\mu = 1/2 vanishes boundaries. dispersion ϕ\phi σ2\sigma^2 parameter Barndorff-Nielsen Jørgensen (1991).  Smaller ϕ\phi concentrates mass around mean; changing μ\mu shifts skews density.
code: grid <- seq(0.001, 0.999, length.out = 400) op <- par(mar = c(4, 4, 1, 1)) plot(grid, dsimplex(grid, mu = 0.5, phi = 1), type = "l", lwd = 2,      xlab = "y", ylab = "density", ylim = c(0, 4)) lines(grid, dsimplex(grid, mu = 0.5, phi = 0.3), lwd = 2, lty = 2, col = "#1a5276") lines(grid, dsimplex(grid, mu = 0.3, phi = 1),   lwd = 2, lty = 3, col = "#a93226") legend("topright", bty = "n",        legend = c("mu=0.5, phi=1", "mu=0.5, phi=0.3", "mu=0.3, phi=1"),        lwd = 2, lty = 1:3, col = c("black", "#1a5276", "#a93226")) par(op)

title: Fast simplex regression with variable dispersion
text: fastsimplexreg() models mean dispersion functions covariates. observation ii, Yi∼Simplex(μi,ϕi),g(μi)=xi⊤β,log⁡ϕi=zi⊤γ. Y_i \sim \mathrm{Simplex}(\mu_i, \phi_i), \qquad g(\mu_i) = x_i^\top\beta, \qquad \log\phi_i = z_i^\top\gamma.  dispersion always uses log link (ϕi>0\phi_i > 0). mean link gg can logit, probit, cloglog neglog: Estimation maximum likelihood. log-likelihood, analytic score, native BFGS optimiser, link inverses run C++; per-observation loop can use OpenMP, fit scales large data sets.

title: Fast simplex regression with variable dispersion
text: mean dispersion submodels separated | Formula: y ~ x1 + x2 mean model z1 + z2 dispersion model. Omitting second part, y ~ x1 + x2, gives constant dispersion.
code: fastsimplexreg(y ~ x1 + x2 | z1 + z2, data = dat, link = "logit")

what: 3. A worked example: simulated recovery data
title: Fast simplex regression with variable dispersion
text: simulate data loosely inspired recovery CD34+ cells stem cell transplantation: response rcd bounded recovery rate, covariates ageadj (adjusted age), chemo (chemotherapy indicator) age. model mean recovery rate ageadj chemo, let dispersion depend age: Positive mean coefficients indicate higher recovery adjusted age chemotherapy; negative age coefficient dispersion model means recovery rate less variable older patients. fitted mean dispersion available directly:
code: set.seed(1) n <- 239 sdac <- data.frame(age = round(rnorm(n, 45, 12)),                    ageadj = pmax(0, round(rnorm(n, 8, 6))),                    chemo = rbinom(n, 1, 0.5)) mu <- simplex_linkinv(1.1 + 0.013 * sdac$ageadj + 0.25 * sdac$chemo, "logit") sdac$rcd <- rsimplex(n, mu, exp(2.6 - 0.015 * sdac$age)) head(sdac) #>   age ageadj chemo       rcd #> 1  37      8     1 0.5653849 #> 2  47     12     0 0.9432660 #> 3  35     14     0 0.8502075 #> 4  64      9     0 0.4972992 #> 5  49      3     0 0.7329104 #> 6  35     15     0 0.7458922 fit <- fastsimplexreg(rcd ~ ageadj + chemo | age, data = sdac, link = "logit") summary(fit) #>  #> Call: #> fastsimplexreg(formula = rcd ~ ageadj + chemo | age, data = sdac,  #>     link = "logit") #>  #> Pearson residuals: #>      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  #> -2.65868 -0.42963  0.19730  0.60193  1.15765  #>  #> Coefficients (mean model with logit link): #>             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept) 0.950559   0.109785   8.658  < 2e-16 *** #> ageadj      0.016059   0.009882   1.625 0.104161     #> chemo       0.382829   0.105654   3.623 0.000291 *** #>  #> Coefficients (dispersion model with log link): #>              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)  1.942640   0.376691   5.157 2.51e-07 *** #> age         -0.003534   0.008081  -0.437    0.662     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Log-likelihood: 167.6 | AIC: -325.1 | BIC: -307.7  #> Deviance:   239 | Observations: 239 | Iterations: 11  #> Convergence: 0 - Converged: relative objective tolerance satisfied. head(cbind(mu = fitted(fit), phi = fitted(fit, "dispersion"))) #>             mu      phi #> [1,] 0.8118171 6.121848 #> [2,] 0.7582792 5.909251 #> [3,] 0.7641173 6.165276 #> [4,] 0.7493392 5.564642 #> [5,] 0.7308100 5.867626 #> [6,] 0.7669995 6.165276 confint(fit) #>                    2.5 %     97.5 % #> (Intercept)  0.735385226 1.16573272 #> ageadj      -0.003310232 0.03542846 #> chemo        0.175751463 0.58990640 #> (Intercept)  1.204338590 2.68094164 #> age         -0.019372010 0.01230303

title: Simplex mixed models for nested proportion data
text: Continuous proportions (0,1)(0, 1) frequently nested: repeated measurements within subject, students within schools, plots within sites. observations within cluster correlated, treating independent understates uncertainty can bias mean dispersion estimates. fastsimplexregmixed() extends simplex regression fastsimplexreg() cluster-specific random effect, keeping variable-dispersion structure.

title: Simplex mixed models for nested proportion data
text: observation ii cluster jj, conditionally cluster random effect bj∼Nq(0,Σ)b_j \sim N_q(0, \Sigma), yij∣bj∼Simplex(μij,ϕij),g(μij)=xij⊤β+zij⊤bj,log⁡ϕij=wij⊤γ. y_{ij} \mid b_j \sim \mathrm{Simplex}(\mu_{ij}, \phi_{ij}), \qquad g(\mu_{ij}) = x_{ij}^\top\beta + z_{ij}^\top b_j, \qquad \log\phi_{ij} = w_{ij}^\top\gamma .  fixed effects β\beta (mean) γ\gamma (dispersion) use multi-part Formula fixed-effects fit; qq-dimensional random effect bjb_j enters mean submodel induces within-cluster correlation. mean link gg one logit, probit, cloglog, neglog; dispersion uses log link carries fixed effects . random effects unobserved, likelihood marginal likelihood obtained integrating , ℓ(θ)=∑jlog⁡∫ℝqexp⁡{∑ilog⁡f(yij∣b)}N(b;0,Σ)db, \ell(\theta) = \sum_j \log \int_{\mathbb{R}^q}   \exp\!\Big\{\textstyle\sum_i \log f(y_{ij}\mid b)\Big\}\,   N(b; 0, \Sigma)\, \mathrm{d}b ,  closed form.

title: Simplex mixed models for nested proportion data
text: integral approximated adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature (AGHQ). cluster integrand centred posterior mode rescaled curvature , Gauss-Hermite rule nAGQ points per dimension applied. nAGQ = 1 recovers Laplace approximation; larger nAGQ refines integral, cost nAGQ^q evaluations per cluster. random-effect covariance Σ=DD⊤\Sigma = D D^\top estimated unconstrained log-Cholesky scale, estimate always positive definite. per-cluster inner mode-finding, quadrature analytic score run C++ parallelised clusters OpenMP.

title: Simplex mixed models for nested proportion data
text: Random effects grouping factor supplied random, using lme4-style bar: random = ~ 1 | subject — random intercept; random = ~ 1 + t | subject — random intercept random slope t; random = ~ 0 + t | subject — random slope .

what: 3. A worked example: simulated intraocular gas decay
title: Simplex mixed models for nested proportion data
text: simulate longitudinal data loosely inspired intraocular gas decay retinal surgery: response Gas fraction gas remaining eye, measured repeatedly time 31 patients (variable ID), LogT/LogT2 (log) time since surgery Time raw time. Successive measurements eye correlated — textbook nested structure. gas fraction decays time; model mean decay LogT LogT2, let dispersion depend Time, add random intercept per eye capture -subject heterogeneity: estimated -subject variance group-level random effects: non-negligible random-intercept variance confirms eyes differ systematically baseline gas retention beyond time covariates explain. number groups marginal fit statistics:
code: set.seed(1) J <- 31; nj <- 6; n <- J * nj ID <- rep(seq_len(J), each = nj); Time <- rep(seq_len(nj) * 10, J) LogT <- log(Time); LogT2 <- LogT^2 b <- rnorm(J, 0, 0.8)[ID] mu <- simplex_linkinv(2.5 - 1.2 * LogT + 0.1 * LogT2 + b, "logit") retinal <- data.frame(Gas = rsimplex(n, mu, exp(-0.5)),                       Time = Time, LogT = LogT, LogT2 = LogT2, ID = ID) retinal$ID <- factor(retinal$ID) str(retinal) #> 'data.frame':    186 obs. of  5 variables: #>  $ Gas  : num  0.516 0.288 0.336 0.23 0.286 ... #>  $ Time : num  10 20 30 40 50 60 10 20 30 40 ... #>  $ LogT : num  2.3 3 3.4 3.69 3.91 ... #>  $ LogT2: num  5.3 8.97 11.57 13.61 15.3 ... #>  $ ID   : Factor w/ 31 levels "1","2","3","4",..: 1 1 1 1 1 1 2 2 2 2 ... fit <- fastsimplexregmixed(   Gas ~ LogT + LogT2 | Time,   random = ~ 1 | ID,   data = retinal,   link = "logit",   nAGQ = 15,   n_threads = 1 ) summary(fit) #>  #> Call: #> fastsimplexregmixed(formula = Gas ~ LogT + LogT2 | Time, data = retinal,  #>     random = ~1 | ID, link = "logit", nAGQ = 15, n_threads = 1) #>  #> Pearson residuals: #>      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  #> -1.95864 -0.63377 -0.03089  0.51770  2.69270  #>  #> Coefficients (mean model with logit link): #>             Estimate Std. what: Fast Simplex Regression with Variable Dispersion
title: Fast Simplex Regression with Variable Dispersion
text: high-performance implementation simplex regression continuous proportions open interval (0,1)(0, 1), separate submodels mean dispersion, fixed- mixed-effects fitting. numerical core (log-likelihood, analytic score, native BFGS, adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature, density, random generation, prediction) written C++ RcppArmadillo, BLAS/LAPACK optional OpenMP. Documentation website: https://evandeilton.github.io/fastsimplexreg/. fastsimplexreg provides high-performance maximum-likelihood estimation simplex regression models continuous proportions open interval (0,1)(0, 1), following Barndorff-Nielsen Jorgensen (1991). fits separate submodels mean dispersion, Yi∼Simplex(μi,ϕi),g(μi)=𝐱i⊤𝛃,log⁡(ϕi)=𝐳i⊤𝛄, Y_i \sim \mathrm{Simplex}(\mu_i, \phi_i), \qquad g(\mu_i) = \mathbf{x}_i^\top \boldsymbol\beta, \qquad \log(\phi_i) = \mathbf{z}_i^\top \boldsymbol\gamma, density f(y;μ,ϕ)=[2πϕ(y(1−y))3]−1/2exp⁡{−12ϕ(y−μ)2y(1−y)μ2(1−μ)2},0<y<1. f(y; \mu, \phi) = \left[2\pi\phi\,(y(1-y))^3\right]^{-1/2} \exp\!\left\{-\frac{1}{2\phi}\, \frac{(y-\mu)^2}{y(1-y)\,\mu^2(1-\mu)^2}\right\}, \qquad 0 < y < 1. entire numerical hot path – log-likelihood, analytic score, native BFGS optimiser, density, random generation, prediction link inverses – implemented C++ RcppArmadillo, BLAS/LAPACK optional OpenMP parallelism, models scale large data sets.

title: Fast Simplex Regression with Variable Dispersion

title: Simplex Distribution Density — dsimplex
text: Evaluates probability density function simplex distribution Barndorff-Nielsen Jorgensen (1991) mean mu dispersion phi (parameter often written \(\sigma^2\)). density $$f(x; \mu, \phi) = [2\pi\phi\,(x(1-x))^3]^{-1/2}   \exp\!\left\{-\frac{1}{2\phi}\,   \frac{(x-\mu)^2}{x(1-x)\,\mu^2(1-\mu)^2}\right\},   \qquad 0 < x < 1.$$ Values x outside open interval \((0, 1)\) return 0 (-Inf log scale). arguments mu phi recycled x. computation carried C++ may use OpenMP threads.

title: Simplex Distribution Density — dsimplex
code: dsimplex(x, mu, phi, log = FALSE, n_threads = 1L)

title: Simplex Distribution Density — dsimplex
text: x Numeric vector observations. Values must lie strictly inside \((0, 1)\) receive positive density. mu Numeric vector means \((0, 1)\), length one length(x). phi Numeric vector positive dispersion values, length one length(x). log Logical; TRUE, log-densities returned. n_threads Integer number OpenMP threads. Use 0 request threads available backend. Defaults 1L (serial).

text: High-performance maximum-likelihood estimation simplex regression models continuous proportions open interval \((0, 1)\). package supports separate submodels mean dispersion multi-part Formula::Formula interface (y ~ x1 + x2 | z1 + z2), four mean links (logit, probit, cloglog, neglog) log link dispersion. entire numerical hot path – log-likelihood, analytic score, native BFGS optimiser, density, random generation, prediction link inverses – implemented C++ RcppArmadillo, BLAS/LAPACK optional OpenMP parallelism, models scale large data sets.

title: fastsimplexreg: Fast Simplex Regression with Variable Dispersion — fastsimplexreg-package

Theory Dispersion Models. Chapman & Hall, London. Zhang, P., Qiu, Z. Shi, C. (2016). simplexreg: R Package Regression Analysis Proportional Data Using Simplex Distribution. Journal Statistical Software, 71(11), 1–21. doi:10.18637/jss.v071.i11

what: Fit a Fast Simplex Regression with Variable Dispersion — fastsimplexreg
title: Fit a Fast Simplex Regression with Variable Dispersion — fastsimplexreg
text: Fits, maximum likelihood, simplex regression model separate submodels mean dispersion. interface uses multi-part formulas Formula package: y ~ x1 + x2 | z1 + z2 first right-hand side component models mean \(\mu\); second component models dispersion \(\phi\). second component omitted, y ~ x1 + x2, dispersion constant (equivalent | 1). mean supports logit, probit, cloglog neglog links; dispersion uses log link. log-likelihood, analytic score, link inverses BFGS optimiser run entirely C++. Matrix-vector products use Armadillo/BLAS per-observation loop may use OpenMP.

title: Fit a Fast Simplex Regression with Variable Dispersion — fastsimplexreg
code: set.seed(123) n <- 500 dat <- data.frame(x1 = rnorm(n), x2 = rbinom(n, 1, 0.4), z1 = rnorm(n)) mu <- simplex_linkinv(-0.4 + 0.8 * dat$x1 - 0.5 * dat$x2, link = "logit") phi <- exp(-1 + 0.6 * dat$z1) dat$y <- rsimplex(n, mu, phi)  fit <- fastsimplexreg(y ~ x1 + x2 | z1, data = dat, link = "logit",                    n_threads = 1L) summary(fit) #>  #> Call: #> fastsimplexreg(formula = y ~ x1 + x2 | z1, data = dat, link = "logit",  #>     n_threads = 1L) #>  #> Pearson residuals: #>      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  #> -2.46935 -0.70189 -0.06618  0.67738  2.87579  #>  #> Coefficients (mean model with logit link): #>              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept) -0.396982   0.013501  -29.40   <2e-16 *** #> x1           0.806678   0.008785   91.83   <2e-16 *** #> x2          -0.525762   0.021152  -24.86   <2e-16 *** #>  #> Coefficients (dispersion model with log link): #>             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept) -1.04037    0.06326 -16.445   <2e-16 *** #> z1           0.63039    0.06345   9.936   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Log-likelihood: 774.8 | AIC: -1540 | BIC: -1519  #> Deviance:   500 | Observations: 500 | Iterations: 18  #> Convergence: 0 - Converged: relative objective tolerance satisfied.  coef(fit) #> (Intercept)          x1          x2 (Intercept)          z1  #>  -0.3969817   0.8066782  -0.5257617  -1.0403689   0.6303906  head(predict(fit, type = "both")) #>          mu       phi #> 1 0.2996206 0.9318746 #> 2 0.3583206 0.3297141 #> 3 0.7027430 0.4877547 #> 4 0.2961153 0.4043430 #> 5 0.3060928 0.3142088 #> 6 0.7284008 0.3275010

what: Fast Simplex Mixed-Effects Regression with Variable Dispersion — fastsimplexregmixed
title: Fast Simplex Mixed-Effects Regression with Variable Dispersion — fastsimplexregmixed
text: Fits two-level (nested) simplex mixed model continuous proportions open interval \((0, 1)\) maximum marginal likelihood, using adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature (AGHQ). Conditional cluster random effect \(b_j \sim N_q(0, \Sigma)\), $$y_{ij} \mid b_j \sim \mathrm{Simplex}(\mu_{ij}, \phi_{ij}), \quad       g(\mu_{ij}) = x_{ij}^\top\beta + z_{ij}^\top b_j, \quad       \log\phi_{ij} = w_{ij}^\top\gamma.$$ fixed-effects mean dispersion submodels use multi-part Formula interface fastsimplexreg(); random effects grouping factor given random.

title: Fast Simplex Mixed-Effects Regression with Variable Dispersion — fastsimplexregmixed
code: fastsimplexregmixed(   formula,   data,   random,   link = c("logit", "probit", "cloglog", "neglog"),   nAGQ = 11L,   start = NULL,   maxit = 300L,   rel_tol = 1e-09,   grad_tol = 1e-06,   n_threads = 1L,   inference = TRUE,   hessian_rel_step = 1e-05,   inner_maxit = 50L,   inner_tol = 1e-08,   trace = FALSE,   subset = NULL,   na.action = stats::na.omit,   model = TRUE,   x = FALSE,   y = TRUE )

NULL, fast link-specific values used. maxit Maximum number BFGS iterations. rel_tol Relative objective tolerance. grad_tol Infinity-norm gradient tolerance. n_threads Number OpenMP threads (clusters). title: Fast Simplex Mixed-Effects Regression with Variable Dispersion — fastsimplexregmixed
text: marginal likelihood integrates cluster random effects AGHQ (nAGQ points per dimension; nAGQ = 1 gives Laplace approximation). per-cluster inner mode-finding, quadrature analytic score implemented C++ (RcppArmadillo, BLAS) parallelised clusters OpenMP, fit scales large nested data sets. random-effect covariance \(\Sigma = D D^\top\) estimated unconstrained log-Cholesky scale, guaranteeing positive-definite estimate. version supports single grouping factor (two-level nesting), Gaussian random effects mean submodel, fixed-effect (variable) dispersion.

code: set.seed(1) J <- 60; nj <- 8; n <- J * nj dat <- data.frame(   g  = factor(rep(seq_len(J), each = nj)),   x1 = rnorm(n),   z1 = rnorm(n) ) b <- rnorm(J, 0, 0.7)[dat$g] mu <- simplex_linkinv(0.3 - 0.6 * dat$x1 + b, "logit") dat$y <- rsimplex(n, mu, exp(-0.4 + 0.3 * dat$z1)) fit <- fastsimplexregmixed(y ~ x1 | z1, random = ~ 1 | g, data = dat,                            nAGQ = 7, n_threads = 1) summary(fit) #>  #> Call: #> fastsimplexregmixed(formula = y ~ x1 | z1, data = dat, random = ~1 |  #>     g, nAGQ = 7, n_threads = 1) #>  #> Pearson residuals: #>      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  #> -2.44761 -0.63577  0.02067  0.59711  2.73018  #>  #> Coefficients (mean model with logit link): #>             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)  0.16879    0.09652   1.749   0.0803 .   #> x1          -0.59902    0.01612 -37.162   <2e-16 *** #>  #> Coefficients (dispersion model with log link): #>             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept) -0.36203    0.06904  -5.244 1.57e-07 *** #> z1           0.29533    0.06615   4.465 8.02e-06 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Random effects: #> Random effects covariance (group: g) #>             Variance Std.Dev. #> (Intercept)   0.5437   0.7374 #>  #> Log-likelihood: 485.8 | AIC: -961.7 | BIC: -940.8  #> Observations: 480 | Groups: 60 | nAGQ: 7 | Iterations: 17  #> Convergence: 0 - Converged: relative objective tolerance satisfied. what: Extractor Methods for Simplex Regression Fits — simplex_fast-methods
title: Extractor Methods for Simplex Regression Fits — simplex_fast-methods
text: Standard extractor methods objects class "simplex_fast" produced fastsimplexreg(). mirror conventions corresponding generics linear generalised linear models. coef Returns estimated coefficients. model = "" returns full vector, "mean" mean submodel "dispersion" dispersion submodel. vcov Returns variance-covariance matrix estimates. Errors model fitted inference = FALSE. logLik Returns maximised log-likelihood, attributes df (number estimated parameters) nobs, class "logLik". nobs Returns number observations used fit. fitted Returns fitted means (model = "mean") fitted dispersions (model = "dispersion"). residuals Returns residuals. type = "response" gives \(y - \hat\mu\); type = "pearson" gives \((y - \hat\mu) / \sqrt{\hat\phi\, V(\hat\mu)}\) simplex unit variance function \(V(\mu) = \{\mu(1-\mu)\}^3\), .e. first-order dispersion-model approximation \(\mathrm{Var}(Y) \approx \phi\,V(\mu)\); type = "deviance" gives signed deviance residual \(\mathrm{sign}(y-\hat\mu)\sqrt{d(y;\hat\mu)/\hat\phi}\) based simplex unit deviance \(d(y;\mu) = (y-\mu)^2 / \{y(1-y)\mu^2(1-\mu)^2\}\). confint Returns Wald confidence intervals \(\hat\theta \pm z_{1-\alpha/2}\,\mathrm{SE}(\hat\theta)\), using covariance matrix vcov. deviance Returns scaled deviance \(\sum_i d(y_i;\hat\mu_i)/\hat\phi_i\), equal sum squared deviance residuals. model.matrix Returns mean (model = "mean") dispersion (model = "dispersion") design matrix. Requires fit stored design (x = TRUE) model frame (model = TRUE). terms Returns "terms" object mean dispersion submodel. formula Returns (multi-part) model formula. model.frame Returns stored model frame (requires model = TRUE fitting time). update Refits model modified formula /arguments. multi-part (|) structure updated correctly Formula package.

what: Inverse of the Simplex Mean Link — simplex_linkinv
title: Inverse of the Simplex Mean Link — simplex_linkinv
text: Computes mean \(\mu = g^{-1}(\eta)\) linear predictor eta, using C++ backend employed fitting model fastsimplexreg(). Four links supported: logit \(g^{-1}(\eta) = 1 / (1 + e^{-\eta})\). probit \(g^{-1}(\eta) = \Phi(\eta)\). cloglog \(g^{-1}(\eta) = 1 - \exp(-\exp(\eta))\). neglog \(g^{-1}(\eta) = \exp(-\exp(-\eta))\), following definition Zhang et al. (2016).

title: fastsimplexreg 0.2.0
text: New fastsimplexregmixed(): two-level (nested) simplex mixed model variable dispersion, estimated adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature (AGHQ; nAGQ = 1 gives Laplace approximation). Gaussian random effects mean submodel specified lme4-style random = ~ terms | group bar; covariance estimated unconstrained log-Cholesky scale. per-cluster inner mode-finding, quadrature analytic score run C++ parallelised clusters OpenMP. S3 methods class "simplex_fast_mixed": print, summary, coef, ranef, VarCorr (re-exported nlme), vcov, logLik, nobs, ngrps, fitted, residuals, predict (re.form) plot. vignettes now analyse real data: sdac (CD34+ cell recovery) retinal (longitudinal intraocular gas) data sets simplexreg. pkgdown documentation website, GitHub Actions workflows (R-CMD-check, pkgdown, test-coverage), MIT license.

title: fastsimplexreg 0.1.0
text: Initial release. fastsimplexreg() fits simplex regression models separate submodels mean dispersion via multi-part Formula interface (y ~ x1 + x2 | z1 + z2), logit, probit, cloglog neglog mean links log dispersion link. Maximum-likelihood estimation uses analytic score native BFGS optimiser implemented C++ RcppArmadillo, BLAS/LAPACK optional OpenMP parallelism. dsimplex() rsimplex() provide density random generation simplex distribution; simplex_linkinv() exposes mean link inverses. full set S3 methods class "simplex_fast", matching conventions lm/glm/betareg fits: coef(), vcov(), confint() (Wald), logLik() (AIC() BIC() work), nobs(), deviance(), fitted(), residuals() (response, Pearson deviance), predict(), simulate(), model.matrix(), terms(), formula(), model.frame(), update() (multi-part-formula aware), print() summary(). plot() method producing ggplot2 diagnostic panels (residuals vs fitted, normal Q-Q, scale-location, observed vs fitted), optionally combined patchwork installed.
